column1: # Round 9 — Path A quintet citation backfill

column4: new_value
new_value

Extends π0 with MEM layer giving the policy short-term (within-task) + long-term (cross-task, >10-min horizon) memory. Co-trained on robot teleoperation + human video + text. Hierarchical inference: VLA predicts subtasks as text tokens, then executes as action chunks.
Multi-Scale Embodied Memory (short + long episodic)
Generalist robot policy with strong simulation-to-real transfer; memory is implicit in the policy network rather than explicit external store.
Implicit memory is harder to inspect or edit; embodied agent only — not generalizable to LLM agents.
π0.5 blog paper
Treats memories as atomic linkable notes — explicit nod to Zettelkasten knowledge management. Dynamic linking; retroactive memory revision.
Atomic-note / Zettelkasten-style
Adaptive memory with explicit policy for what to remember vs forget.
Research-only; no production deployment.
GitHub WujiangXu/A-mem arxiv 2502.12110 NeurIPS 2025 poster

Clinician-assist ambient documentation. Source mapping: every AI-generated summary element traced back to the source utterance. Audit-and-trust layer over episodic memory. Built on proprietary 1.5M+ medical-encounter dataset.
Grounded-transcript provenance
Strongest published evidence for clinical-encounter memory accuracy; multi-EMR integrations and large hospital deployments.
Enterprise sales motion only; longitudinal cross-visit memory layered on top of single-encounter scribing rather than the architecture's primary unit.
abridge.com
Optimises context compression for long-horizon LLM agents.
Context-compression optimisation
Agent-context-orchestration memory.
Preprint; orchestration overhead in agent loops.

Deep Memory feature optimises embedding space per use-case (+22% retrieval accuracy). Deep Lake PG unifies serverless Postgres (agent short-term state) + billion-scale vector search (long-term memory).
Multimodal vector + serverless Postgres
Multi-modal data lake (vectors + images + video) with version control — strong for ML data pipelines plus memory.
Less developer-API-friendly than Pinecone / Weaviate; positioning straddles ML training and runtime memory.
activeloop.ai

Runtime enforcement targeting memory poisoning, unauthorised execution, identity spoofing per the OWASP LLM threat list. Visibility/control over MCP servers and locally-installed AI tools — the infrastructure layer where memory is most exposed.
MCP / Shadow-AI runtime enforcement
Enterprise DLP for AI products — protects against data exfiltration via memory writes/reads; backed by Proofpoint's enterprise channel.
Enterprise-tier pricing and complexity; smaller mind-share than Lakera or Microsoft Defender for AI.
acuvity.ai Proofpoint acquisition
Smaller classifier LM predicts query complexity, then routes to no-retrieval / single-step / iterative retrieval as appropriate. NAACL 2024.
Query-complexity routing
Query-adaptive routing across retrieval strategies — picks the right method per question.
Routing decisions can be wrong; adds inference cost.
GitHub starsuzi/Adaptive-RAG arxiv 2403.14403 ACL Anthology (NAACL)
Leverages cross-domain experience for agentic problem solving.
Cross-domain experience KB
Knowledge-base memory for agent task experience.
Preprint; KB curation overhead.
Open agentic framework that uses computers like a human.
Open agentic computer-use framework
Computer-use agent with explicit screen-history memory.
Preprint; screen-history scales aggressively.
Workflow-based memory framework component.
Workflow as memory
Workflow-pattern abstraction means agents recall multi-step procedures, not just facts.
Workflow-shaped scope; less applicable to free-form recall.
OpenReview
Towards an efficient self-evolving agent system.
Efficient self-evolving agent system
Agent that evolves its own memory architecture.
Preprint; evolutionary stability concerns.
Long-horizon web agents with proactive context management.
Proactive context management
Folded-context agent — compresses old context into summary memory.
Preprint; folding-quality bounds long-horizon recall.
Learning unified long-term and short-term memory management.
Unified short + long-term management
Agent-shaped memory with first-class read/write operations exposed to the agent loop.
Preprint; agent-controlled memory quality bounds value.
Extracts, stores, adapts, and reuses structured plan templates from planning stages of agent applications. NeurIPS 2025 poster.
Test-time plan-template memory
Cache-aware planning reduces repeated work for similar tasks.
Niche use case; cache-invalidation issues common in evolving domains.
NeurIPS 2025 poster

When Mem0 is connected, gains Memory Operation Timeline, Search Analytics, Memory Growth tracking, Error Tracking per memory call. Standalone, records context at each step but doesn't analyse memory quality.
Operational metrics for agent memory
Agent-loop observability with strong memory-step instrumentation; integrates with Mem0.
Agent-orchestration tilted; less broad than Galileo / Langfuse for general LLM observability.
agentops.ai

AGENTS.md at repo root (and ~/.codex/AGENTS.md for global); hierarchical concatenation from root to cwd. Persists build/test commands, conventions, architecture overview, security constraints, git workflow.
Cross-tool open standard
Cross-vendor convention — the closest thing to a universal agent memory file format; works across Claude Code, Cursor, and Codex CLI.
Adoption is informal (no spec authority); agent-by-agent interpretation differences mean behavior isn't fully portable.
agents.md GitHub Blog

Open-source AI automation platform. Routes between short-term and long-term memory adaptively across any LLM provider; plugin system for storage backends. Memory managed at the instruction-management layer — task context, instruction state, conversation history as unified agent state.
Adaptive provider-spanning + plugin storage
OSS adaptive memory with web-search fallback when local memory misses; multi-LLM support.
Smaller community; documentation uneven; production track record limited.
GitHub Josh-XT/AGiXT

Agno (formerly Phidata). AgentStorage persists sessions to a DB; AgentMemory auto-classifies/store user preferences and conversation summaries. Single-line integrations with LanceDB, Pinecone, Weaviate, Qdrant.
AgentStorage + AgentMemory + DBs
Postgres + pgvector default — operational simplicity for shops that already run Postgres.
Less first-class memory abstraction than dedicated memory layers; Agno is broader-than-memory framework.
GitHub agno-agi/agno memory docs agno.com
Life-long personalisation of LLMs.
Life-long LLM personalisation
Persona-grounded memory model for personalized agents.
Preprint; persona drift concerns over long sessions.

SEA-LION-Embedding (March 2026): retrieval + reranking models contrastively trained on 245M text pairs across 10 SE Asian languages. SEA-BED benchmark (169 datasets). SEA-LION v4 (Gemma-based) at 128K context with native function calling.
SE-Asian cross-lingual semantic-retrieval memory
First Southeast-Asian-language-tuned memory model; strong with Bahasa, Thai, Vietnamese.
Regional scope limits global relevance; smaller English benchmarks coverage.
sea-lion.ai

Appends conversation turns to .aider.chat.history.md in project root. restore_chat_history defaults to false — no automatic replay into new sessions. Community /session save command for JSON state snapshots. .aider.conf.yml carries static preferences.
Per-repo chat history file + YAML config
Most established CLI coding agent; explicit project-level conventions file + repo map keeps memory inspectable.
CLI-only — no IDE integration; memory is inspectable but not auto-learned.
aider.chat FAQ
CONVENTIONS.md (any name, loaded via --read or read: in config); .aider.conf.yml (home / git-root / cwd, last wins); .aiderignore (gitignore-syntax).
Multi-file convention pattern
Plain-text + git-versioned conventions file — the most CLAUDE.md-like memory pattern outside Anthropic's tools.
Aider-only; manual curation required; no semantic recall.
conventions docs YAML config

Global Workspace Layer combining low-rank explicit memory + bottleneck attention + Hopfield-style associative memory. Decouples memory from the Hopfield network via learnable explicit memory. Outperforms sparse-Transformer baselines on Sort-of-CLEVR. Latest revision Mar 2025.
Hopfield + low-rank explicit memory
Associative-memory model integrated into transformer attention.
Research-stage; training stability concerns.

German enterprise stack. PhariaCatch captures + preserves organisational knowledge through structured human-machine collaboration; PhariaAssistant queries via semantic search. Combines sovereign deployment constraints with explainability tooling.
Sovereign governed-document memory
European data-residency platform with explicit memory tooling for regulated industries.
Mostly EU-public-sector positioning; minimal North American adoption.
PhariaAI
NeuralSearch combines vector + keyword via neural hashing — compresses to 1/10th size while retaining 99% info. AI-powered personalisation + recommendations.
Neural-hash hybrid (vector + keyword)
Lowest-latency hosted search at developer-friendly pricing; NeuralSearch adds vector layer without sacrificing keyword speed.
Indexed-content-volume pricing scales aggressively; less suited to large enterprise corpora than Glean.
algolia.com
Generalist agent enabling scalable agentic reasoning.
Generalist agentic reasoning
Architecture for life-long task agents.
Preprint; life-long claims need long evaluations.
Self-evolving generative agent for agent generation.
Self-evolving generative agent
Alita extension with grounded memory.
Preprint; grounding quality varies by domain.
RDF triple/quad store with RDFS++ / OWL reasoning. v8.4 (May 2025) added an NLQ interface.
RDF triple/quad store + reasoning
FedShard sharding architecture handles trillion-triple scale; strong RDF + reasoning support for semantic-web workloads.
Niche product with a small community; tooling and docs lag mass-market alternatives.
allegrograph.com docs
Null-space-constrained knowledge editing for language models.
Null-space knowledge editing
DeepMind weight-editing approach with strong knowledge-locality preservation.
Research-only; eval primarily on synthetic edits; production fine-tuning workflows still preferred.
Vector index on graph nodes queryable via openCypher. Mem0 integration GA 2025; Cognee integration for agentic RAG. Combines semantic recall with multi-hop traversal in one managed service.
Vector + graph in one managed service
Managed graph database with vector + graph hybrid for AWS-native architectures; first-class integration with Bedrock and SageMaker.
AWS-only; vector + graph hybrid is newer than dedicated competitors; pricing complexity around graph queries.
neptune
Tags + bidirectional links + task/calendar integration. "Task Score" priority matrix. AmpleAI plugin supports OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Grok, DeepSeek, Ollama.
Tags + bidirectional + Task Score
Linked-note PKM with task management built in; AI integrations cover memory across notes and tasks.
Smaller user base than Obsidian / Notion; AI features less prominent.
amplenote.com
Native graph-based memory for autonomous AI agents. Bio-inspired sleep-based memory consolidation.
Bio-inspired sleep consolidation
Hippocampus-inspired neural memory model — explicit episodic encoding and consolidation.
Research-oriented; production deployment limited.
GitHub ldclabs/anda-hippocampus

Replaces MLP layers with sparse cross-layer transcoders to produce "replacement models" where activations correspond to interpretable features; traces attribution graphs showing which features causally influenced which outputs. Includes case studies on factual recall, revealing how facts are stored and retrieved from weights at the circuit level. Open-source tools run on any open-weights model.
Mechanistic interpretability of parametric storage
Only published tool that makes parametric (weight-level) memory storage mechanistically interpretable rather than behaviorally probed; attribution graphs are auditable per-prompt.
Not a memory system — it studies memory; findings are descriptive, not prescriptive; scalability to full production Claude models is limited (primary validation on 18-layer models and Haiku).
methods paper Anthropic blog open-source release
Memory tool API + consumer "Memory" feature + Auto Dream consolidation. Document-as-memory rather than extracted-fact-as-memory.
File-backed + tool-driven + Auto Dream
Three-tier model (memory tool API + consumer Memory + Auto Dream) covers developer, user, and system layers; document-as-memory unit aligns with how humans organize information.
Three-tier model means developers and users see different abstractions; Auto Dream consolidation is not user-controllable.
memory tool docs Auto Dream guide memory import tool Wiegold guide

Mounts memory as files on a managed filesystem; Claude reads and writes via the same bash/code-execution tools used for agentic tasks. Unit of storage is a file — inspectable, editable, exportable by developers. Full audit log per session. Memory beta launched April 23, 2026, two weeks after Managed Agents itself launched April 8.
Filesystem-backed cross-session persistent memory
Filesystem metaphor aligns with Claude's existing tool-use primitives; no separate embedding pipeline; human-auditable without special tooling.
Memory granularity and eviction policy not yet documented publicly; filesystem-as-memory has known scalability limits at large knowledge volumes; beta status means API surface may change.
Managed Agents docs Techzine TestingCatalog
openCypher over Postgres. Pairs with pgvector for graph + vector hybrid retrieval. Azure Database for PostgreSQL ships AGE with ai_extension LLM functions for entity extraction directly in SQL.
PostgreSQL graph extension
Postgres extension that adds OpenCypher graph queries — KG without leaving Postgres.
Performance plateaus at large graph sizes; ecosystem of integrations smaller than Neo4j.
age.apache.org
Folder + tag hierarchy; iCloud sync. Writing Tools (proofread / rewrite / summarise) across all note content; audio transcription + AI summary; smart search; reminders from to-dos.
Folder + tag hierarchy + Writing Tools
On-device intelligence keeps personal-note context private; integrates across Apple's notes, mail, calendar memory.
Apple-only; AI feature set lags GPT-class competitors and is conservative about cross-app inference.
apple.com/apple-intelligence
HybridGraphRAG combines vector search, graph traversal, full-text in one AQL query. ArangoGraphML for ML pipelines; LangChain integration.
Multi-model: graph + doc + KV + vector
Multi-model (graph + document + KV) in one engine — useful when memory crosses paradigms without managing multiple databases.
Multi-model adds complexity vs single-purpose engines; smaller community than Neo4j or MongoDB.
arangodb.com

Episodic observations as timestamped vertices; LLM extracts relational triplets into a semantic knowledge graph; episodic edges connect semantic nodes — structural equivalent of hippocampal index binding cortical representations. Dual-layer world model for interactive text agents.
CLS dual-layer episodic + semantic KG
Graph-based reasoning memory — explicit knowledge structure during reasoning.
Research-stage; graph build cost limits real-time use.
Associative Recurrent Memory Transformer. Stores tokens in Hopfield-style energy basins for constant-time pattern completion.
Hopfield-style energy basins
Associative memory built into Recurrent Memory Transformer.
Preprint; training stability of associative-recurrent hybrid.

Treats memory as first-class architecture. Captures the digital footprint of every interaction; retrieves preference and history at engagement time. Public example: airline knowing a frequent flyer wants aisle seats with her son — preference-aware, not just history-lookup.
Long-term interaction memory + preference
Long-running CX vendor pivoting hard into generative — memory grounded in years of contact-center data.
Tightly coupled to ASAPP's CX platform; less open than newer entrants.
asapp.com
Meta AI. Jointly fine-tunes retriever + LM for few-shot tasks.
Few-shot retrieval-augmented LM
Meta retrieval architecture for few-shot learning.
Research scope; less production utility than fine-tuned RAG.

Long-term memory module that learns to memorise the context at test time. Omega rule (sliding-window update over all past tokens), polynomial feature mappings, Muon optimiser. Generalises to "DeepTransformers" family. May 2025.
Omega rule + polynomial features + Muon
Long-context atlas of attention patterns.
Preprint; attention-pattern reuse across tasks unclear.

Real-time knowledge graph indexed from connected repos + commit history + CI/CD + tickets + docs. Separate Agent Memory layer (Sept 2025) captures session decisions/corrections/goals as discrete items with developer review/approval UI. MCP endpoint exposes the graph.
Real-time semantic Context Engine + curated Agent Memory
Codebase-level semantic graph (Context Engine) gives precise navigation across very large repos that confuse generic file-context tools.
Heavyweight indexing makes initial setup slow on large codebases; pricing is enterprise-tier.
augmentcode.com Context Engine Agent Memory review
ListMemory chronological context + teachable agents that vectorise corrections. Integrates with Mem0/Zep rather than building deep memory natively.
List + teachable vectors
Microsoft Research origin; strong multi-agent conversation memory model.
AutoGen's broader feature drift over time has affected memory abstractions; less stable than dedicated layers.
GitHub microsoft/autogen

Tsinghua spinout. Foundation agent series for autonomous smartphone/PC GUI control. Persistent task memory retains shopping history, app usage patterns, multi-step task state across sessions. AutoGLM-Thinking claimed first agent capable of concurrent thinking and acting.
GUI agent + cross-session task memory
Mobile / desktop autonomous agent with memory tuned for multi-step UI automation.
Closed product; primarily Chinese-market; documentation in Chinese.
project page Open-AutoGLM

Applies AutoML to RAG: greedy algorithm evaluates and selects best-performing module at each pipeline stage (query expansion, retrieval, reranking, generation). Validated on ARAGOG (423 papers, 107 QAs).
AutoML-style greedy RAG pipeline
Automatic RAG pipeline tuning across many configurations.
Preprint; tuning compute cost.
AWS managed memory service for agents running on Bedrock AgentCore. Native integrations with LangChain/LangGraph and Strands. Drives long-running MCP servers + agent workloads.
Managed memory in Bedrock AgentCore
Native to Bedrock with deep integration into IAM, KMS, CloudWatch — enterprise compliance posture is built in.
AWS-only; pricing tied to Bedrock; cross-cloud portability requires rework.
AgentCore Memory docs long-running MCP + Strands

Extends 20 bAbI reasoning tasks (fact chaining, induction, deduction, counting, lists) by scattering relevant sentences across PG-19 book text. Scales to 10M tokens. Tests whether models can chain distributed facts, not merely retrieve a single needle.
Distributed multi-fact reasoning across long docs
Extends bAbI tasks to very long context — clean methodology heritage.
Synthetic; doesn't capture real-world memory complexity.
arxiv 2406.10149 GitHub booydar
Agent memory product evaluated against LoCoMo.
Memory + LoCoMo evaluation harness
Lightweight managed memory layer with simple SDK; quick to integrate into greenfield agents.
Newer entrant; thin track record; small team.
GitHub Backboard-io benchmark repo
Hierarchical "OS" with Storage / Updating / Retrieval / Generation modules. Short-term → mid-term via FIFO dialogue-chain; mid-term → long-term via segmented paging.
Hierarchical: short / mid / long-term
ICML 2025 venue — peer-reviewed memory-as-OS framework.
Research scope; smaller community than commercial memory layers.
GitHub BAI-LAB/MemoryOS ACL Anthology arxiv 2506.06326

Small wearable (pendant / clip / bracelet). Continuous capture; converts speech to text immediately; never stores raw audio . Daily summaries, personal fact sheet, to-do suggestions. Cloud processing on derived text only.
Audio pendant — never stores raw audio
Never stores raw audio — derived-text-only architecture is the strongest privacy model in the wearable category; Amazon acquisition gives runway.
Cloud-only processing; depends on Amazon's roadmap post-acquisition.
bee.computer Amazon acquisition

Target identification / drug repurposing / mechanism tracing. 85+ data sources, petabyte-scale, rebuilt every few weeks. Wet-lab results re-enter the graph and shift downstream predictions — institutional experimental memory closing a feedback loop.
Continuously-refreshed biological KG
Long-running drug-discovery KG with established pharma partnerships — memory is paired with experimental decision support, not just retrieval.
Drug-discovery scope only; commercial/subscription model; investor-facing pivots have introduced strategic uncertainty.
benevolent.com

Argues standard RAG breaks on bounded agent conversation streams (highly correlated, redundant spans). Decomposes the stream into semantic units, organises them into themes, then inverts that structure for retrieval.
Decoupled-aggregate memory retrieval
Position paper arguing RAG is insufficient for agent memory.
Position paper; no concrete method.

Single embedding model supporting dense, sparse, and multi-vector (ColBERT-style) retrieval simultaneously. 100+ languages, inputs up to 8192 tokens. Self-knowledge distillation across the three modes.
Unified dense-sparse-multi-vector embedding
Multi-lingual, multi-functionality, multi-granularity embedding model.
Preprint; embedding-model rather than full RAG architecture.
In-browser WebContainer (Rust-backed shared memory) is the implicit state store. No dedicated memory abstraction — project files are the state. Team plan adds shared workspaces + GitHub integration.
WebContainer file system
WebContainer-based agent — full-stack apps run in the browser; memory is grounded in actual runnable state.
Browser-runtime-only; not applicable to native or backend-heavy projects.
bolt.new GitHub
Per-plan vector-DB storage quota + LLMz autonomous engine (in-session working memory) + Knowledge Base (semantic search over uploaded docs). Long-term user memory persists across sessions.
Vector DB + LLMz engine + KB
Focused on conversational bot deployments at scale — memory is structured around conversation lifecycle (handoff, escalation, return).
Bot-builder market positioning narrows applicability; less relevant for general agentic apps.
KB docs

Privacy-focused integrated assistant. Memory is user-curated: explicit facts and preferences persist across all sessions, stored locally with no server retention. Agentic browsing experiment (Brave Nightly Dec 2025) takes multi-step actions inside sites referencing those preferences.
User-curated local-only memory
Privacy-first browser AI with memory bounded by Brave's no-tracking commitment; on-device model option.
Brave-browser-only; smaller user base than Edge / Chrome; AI features lag market leaders.
brave.com/leo
Builds web agents with human-inspired web-browsing actions.
Human-inspired web browsing
Browser-task agent with explicit memory of past navigation patterns.
Preprint; browser-state drift breaks long-term memory.

Cloud browser infrastructure for AI agents. Headless Chromium sessions with persistent cookies, localStorage, session recording — agent workflows carry state across runs. Memory at the session/automation tier rather than conversational.
Programmatic session persistence (dev infra)
Most developer-friendly path to remote browser agents — memory is structured around session replays, not chat history.
Infrastructure-tier product; not useful directly to end users without an agent layer on top.
browserbase.com
Thought-augmented reasoning with LLMs. NeurIPS 2024.
Thought-augmented reasoning
Thought-augmented reasoning — caches and reuses high-level thoughts across queries; NeurIPS 2024.
Buffer maintenance overhead; thought-quality bounds reuse value.
NeurIPS proceedings
Agent-memory product. LLM-curated hierarchical context.
Agent memory product
Coding-focused memory layer with explicit IDE plugin distribution — meets developers where they work.
Coding-agent scope; less applicable as general agent memory.
byterover.dev LoCoMo benchmark post

"Don't Do RAG." Preloads all relevant documents into context, caches the resulting KV state; at inference the model answers from cache with no retrieval step — eliminates retrieval latency + retrieval-error failure modes.
KV-cache preloading replaces retrieval
Prefill-cache reuse — when the corpus fits in context, retrieval can be replaced with cache lookup.
Requires the corpus to fit in context; doesn't scale to large knowledge bases.
Constructivist view of agentic memory for reading comprehension.
Constructivist agentic memory
Compressed associative memory with retrieval.
Preprint; compression-recall tradeoff not fully characterized.

Object-graph (people, books, projects as typed objects); daily notes; bidirectional links. AI chat grounded in current note; AI property auto-fill; in-editor writing assistant; pattern-finding across objects.
Object-graph PKM (typed objects)
Object-database PKM model — typed entities (people, books, projects) make memory queries semantic by construction.
Niche product; smaller user base; AI features less developed than Notion.
capacities.io

Drug discovery / target identification / causal mechanism tracing. The graph is the memory: 7 years of curated biomedical cause-effect relationships compounding with each new ingestion. Scientific RAG retrieves from a versioned causal substrate.
Persistent causal knowledge graph
Causal-biology graph extracted from full literature corpus is unique — most science-AI competitors retrieve documents rather than causal links.
Biology / pharma scope; not general scientific search; subscription pricing.
causaly.com
Context-Dependent Memory framework. Three-stage encoding (expert / short-term / long-term) + graph-structured context-dependent indexing. NAACL 2025 Industry.
3-stage encoding + graph indexing
Cross-domain memory sharing — agents transfer learned memory across tasks.
Research-stage; cross-domain transfer brittle.
ACL Anthology (NAACL 2025) PDF

Korean startup. Connects scattered internal/external data across multiple chats and services to improve AI answer quality at retrieval. 18-year-old CEO Jang Jun-ha; pitch is that answer quality depends on what the model remembers, not which model.
Cross-service context stitching / retrieval memory
Cell-based memory abstraction — discrete units with provenance; novel mental model.
New abstraction means learning curve; small adoption.
WOWTALE coverage

Chat Memories (user-defined facts), auto-memories for c.ai+ subscribers, pinned memories, in-context retention. PipSqueak 2 model (April 2026) reduces in-conversation drift. Memory Visualization meter forthcoming.
Layered: chat memories + auto-memories + pinned
Largest user base in the character-chat category — billions of messages, deep memory of long-running character relationships.
Memory model is opaque to users and devs; cross-platform export is not supported; recent strategic pivots add uncertainty.
character.ai

No-code interactive narrative platform. Characters track what they know, when they were lied to, what they misremember; story state changes downstream behaviour. Characters can deliberately lie / misremember and track when players have lied.
Author-controlled memory nodes + lying / misremembering
Story-graph memory model — character memory is structured around narrative arcs, not just chat turns; well-suited to interactive fiction.
Narrative-game scope; smaller game-engine integration story than Inworld.
charisma.ai

Activates a Socratic interaction style — guiding questions, scaffolded concepts, knowledge checks rather than direct answers. With ChatGPT Memory enabled, persists learning goals and facts across sessions. Memory is not study-mode-specific; it is the same general ChatGPT Memory, so personalization depth depends on whether the user has memory enabled and populated.
Socratic mode + general ChatGPT Memory
Only product in this set built on a general-purpose model with cross-domain breadth — student can study any subject without vendor having pre-scoped curriculum; lowest friction (free tier).

Implemented as custom system instructions rather than trained behavior — OpenAI describes it as a temporary iterative approach; educational memory is a thin layer over general ChatGPT Memory, not a purpose-built learner model.
OpenAI Study Mode FAQ Axios
Limited native hybrid (users build RRF custom). Fast Rust core (v2.5).
Embedded vector DB (Rust core)
Lowest-friction local vector store — embedded mode means zero ops for prototyping; popular default in agent tutorials.
Production scale-out story is less mature than Weaviate / Qdrant; managed cloud (Chroma Cloud) is newer.
trychroma.com GitHub chroma-core/chroma
Semantic chunking divides documents into coherent sections; LLM-based relevance scorer filters each chunk against the query before generation, pruning irrelevant chunks and reducing hallucination.
LLM-scored chunk-level filtering
Chunk-level RAG with adaptive chunk sizing.
Preprint; chunk-size optimization is task-specific.
Comprehensive survey. ACM Computing Surveys 2025. Anchor reference for the continual-learning-of-LLMs subfield.
Continual learning of LLMs (survey)
Comprehensive survey of continual learning specifically for LLMs.
Survey-only; no new method; recommendations are general.
CSUR 2025 survey repo
Bibliographic metadata curation (Web of Science, Derwent, Cortellis). Human editorial governance + journal-deindexing. Memory-adjacent — included as a curated-knowledge baseline.
Curated bibliographic metadata
Curated bibliographic knowledge graph (Web of Science, Cortellis) — ground-truth memory for science / pharma / IP search.
Subscription cost is high; coverage is opinionated by editorial scope; not a developer-facing API for agent integration.
clarivate.com

Claude Code plugin packaging of Memvid (see Dedicated memory layers). Wraps the Memvid .mv2 single-file store as a slash-command-installable plugin so per-project memory becomes a git-committable file inside the repo.
Memvid → Claude Code plugin
Claude Code plugin that bundles Memvid as the per-project memory store; git-committable single-file.
Plugin layer on top of Memvid — adds another moving part for the same underlying engine.
GitHub memvid/claude-brain
Persistent file-based memory at ~/.claude/projects/<project>/memory/ with a MEMORY.md index plus typed memory files (user / feedback / project / reference). Loaded into context across sessions.
Per-project file directory
Per-project automatic memory built into the runtime — no plugin install, no external store, no extra prompts.
Single-vendor lock-in to Claude Code; no inspect/edit UI for what's been auto-saved; opaque to other tools.
Claude Code memory docs
FastAPI server. RBAC + API-key auth, encryption, backup/recovery, embedding pipeline with deduplication. Only server in this list with role-based access control over pgvector.
PostgreSQL + pgvector with RBAC
Cross-tool MCP that bridges Claude Code and Cursor memory — useful when you switch between IDEs.
Niche use case; small user base; setup involved.
GitHub Angleito

Markdown file injected at session start; reloaded post-compaction. Document-as-memory: human-readable, version-controlled, no extraction or vector store. Anthropic-canonical pattern; equivalent emerges in Cursor / Windsurf.
Git-backed persistent instructions
Plain-text + git-versioned + human-reviewable — the highest-trust memory format with zero infra; works in any editor or terminal.
Manual curation required; doesn't scale beyond ~10–20 KB before context bloat; no semantic recall, only injection-by-default.
Claude Code memory docs

Hooks into Claude Code's session lifecycle; compresses tool outputs into ~500-token observations via Claude's agent SDK. SQLite + FTS5 with optional Chroma vector. "Endless Mode" compresses in-flight to extend session length. Captures what the agent did , not just what the user typed.
Episodic / session-log compression
OSS plugin that adds auto-memory + indexing without sending data to Anthropic's cloud — strongest privacy posture for Claude Code memory.
Plugin install + maintenance burden; less polished than first-party auto-memory.
GitHub thedotmack/claude-mem
Wraps Supermemory cloud API. Team memory shared across project members, separate from personal memories. Auto-capture on session end; per-repo config.
Team-scope memory plugin
Claude Code plugin packaging Supermemory for per-project memory.
Plugin-distributed product layered on a smaller memory layer than Mem0 / Graphiti.
GitHub supermemoryai/claude-supermemory

.clinerules single file or .clinerules/ directory of numbered .md files (e.g. 01-style.md ). Global at ~/.cline/rules/ . Files version-controlled and AI-editable — Cline can write back. Toggleable per-session via UI.
Version-controlled, AI-editable rules
Cline IDE plugin's rule file — git-versioned, IDE-aware; auto-loaded per project.
Cline-only; rule file maintenance burden; no auto-learning.
Cline blog docs

memory-bank/ directory of Markdown: projectBrief.md , productContext.md , activeContext.md , systemPatterns.md , techContext.md , progress.md . Loaded in full at start of every task. Compensates for context-window reset between sessions.
Session-spanning state pattern
Structured memory bank pattern (multiple files) — explicit decomposition of project context across 6 canonical docs.
Cline-only convention; community pattern, not a tool — discipline-dependent.
Memory Bank docs

Two complementary NAVER Labs Europe papers (Jan 2025). Provence fine-tunes DeBERTa as a sequence labeler to dynamically detect needed pruning amount per query and mask irrelevant context spans before generation, unifying pruning with reranking in one pass. PISCO is a distillation-only approach (no annotated data, no pretraining) that trains an LLM to decode from a 16x-compressed document representation.
RAG context pruning + soft compression
PISCO requires no labeled data or pretraining — distillation from open-ended questions only, cheap to adapt to new domains.
Provence relies on a separately-trained DeBERTa ranker (extra pipeline component); PISCO requires fine-tuning the target LLM, so does not apply zero-shot to arbitrary models.
v5.2 adds graph-vector-text hybrid retrieval natively. MCP server for Claude/GPT agents. GraphRAG in three lines of code.
Graph + vector + text hybrid retrieval
Strong at trillion-edge scale; popular in China for fraud / social-network workloads.
Heavy operational overhead; documentation in English lags Chinese resources.

Places a known sentence ("needle") at varying positions inside a long irrelevant document ("haystack") and prompts the model to retrieve it. Tests context lengths from 2K to model max. Greg Kamradt, 2023, no formal venue.

nomi.ai

Notion's first-party AI. Reads up to 50 pages per query (as of Jan 2026); Custom Instructions persist brand voice / team structure / preferences. Context follows you across model swaps. Notably not persistent cross-session learning — it organises and summarises what's in Notion rather than learning from interactions.

Dual-system (VLM "System 2" + Diffusion Transformer "System 1") jointly trained end-to-end; planner outputs persist as conditioning context across action generation. N1.7 adopts relative end-effector action space — incremental spatial memory via positional history.

arxiv 2503.14734 GitHub

Interleaves sparse MoE layers with Mamba-2 layers; Mamba layers maintain a fixed-size recurrent state (constant memory during generation) rather than a growing KV cache. Attention layers use GQA with only 2 KV heads. Two parallel memory systems: explicit small KV cache (attention layers only) and implicit compressed history in the Mamba state. Post-trained via multi-environment RL for multi-step tool use.
Hybrid Mamba + sparse KV-cache, 1M-token context
Constant-memory Mamba state eliminates KV-cache explosion for the majority of layers, enabling genuinely long-context inference without proportional memory growth.
Mamba state is a lossy compressed summary — information not captured is lost; memory-relevant properties not benchmarked against dedicated memory-augmented LLMs on QA or agent tasks.
arxiv 2512.20856 NVIDIA page HF model card

Builds long-horizon memory by captioning video segments with VILA, storing captions with timestamps + 3D position coordinates in MilvusDB. At query time, LLM iterates retrieval across text, time, and position modalities. Deployed on Nova Carter robot (Jetson Orin).
Spatio-temporal episodic store (vector DB + coordinates)

NVIDIA-stack-tilted; research-stage.

Continuous Thought Machines. Neural synchronization across neurons is used directly as a latent representation. Distinct from Sakana NAMM (evolutionary attention memory) — CTM is an architectural rethink of the recurrent state itself.
